evals.prompt: Create a diagram for my YouTube video about setting up cold email infrastructure. The video covers: buying domains, setting up mailboxes with Google Workspace, warming them up for 2 weeks, testing deliverability, then scaling to 1000+ emails/day.
evals.expected_output: Flowchart with 4-8 nodes using shorthand labels (e.g. '3 Domains', 'Warmup 2wk'), diamond for decision point, valid JSON spec, no complete sentences

assertions.text: Diagram type chosen is appropriate (flowchart or process, NOT comparison)
All labels are 4 words or fewer
No complete sentences in any label
Uses shorthand/abbreviations (e.g. '2 wks' not 'two weeks')
Valid JSON spec with required fields (type, nodes, edges)
4-8 nodes total (not too sparse, not cluttered)
No banned AI words (delve, leverage, seamless, etc.)
Logical flow — edges create a sensible progression
Key steps from brief are all represented (domains, mailboxes, warmup, test, scale)
Title is shorthand (under 5 words)
No decorative elements or unnecessary nodes
Node shapes are semantically appropriate (diamonds for decisions only)

evals.prompt: I'm explaining in my course why AI-powered outreach beats manual outreach. Manual: 50 emails/day, generic templates, no personalization, costs $4k/mo for SDR. AI: 1000+ emails/day, hyper-personalized, costs $300/mo, runs 24/7. Make a diagram.
evals.expected_output: Comparison diagram with two columns, red for manual / green for AI, stats preserved ($4k, $300, 50, 1000+), shorthand labels, symbols (✓, ✗)

assertions.text: Diagram type is comparison (not flowchart) — this is clearly a vs. topic
Two distinct columns/sides visible in spec (group left/right or similar)
All labels 4 words or fewer
Numbers/stats from brief preserved ($4k, $300, 50, 1000+)
Uses symbols (→, $, ✓, ✗) to convey meaning without words
Valid JSON spec with required fields
Both sides have roughly equal number of items (within 1)
Color used to differentiate sides (e.g. red for manual, green for AI)
No banned AI words
Shorthand copy style — fragments not sentences
Captures the core contrast (cost, volume, personalization)
Title uses shorthand (under 5 words)

evals.prompt: Diagram for my YouTube video explaining my content strategy. Central idea: Content Engine. Three pillars: Educational (tutorials, how-tos), Authority (case studies, results), Engagement (polls, stories, replies). Each pillar feeds back into lead generation. Show how they connect.
evals.expected_output: Concept map or hierarchy with center node 'Content Engine', 3 branches with sub-items, feedback arrows to lead gen, 8-15 nodes, color per pillar

assertions.text: Diagram type is concept-map or hierarchy (radial structure, not linear)
Central/root node is 'Content Engine' or similar shorthand
Three branches visible (Educational, Authority, Engagement)
Sub-items under each branch (tutorials, case studies, polls, etc.)
All labels 4 words or fewer
Uses shorthand (not 'Educational Content' → just 'Educational' or 'Edu')
Valid JSON spec with required fields
Lead generation connection shown (feedback loop or arrows back)
No banned AI words
8-15 nodes total (enough detail without clutter)
Color used to distinguish the 3 pillars
Concept relationships are meaningful (not just random connections)
